Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Continued To Feb-05-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Dec-18-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memorandum In Support
Off Calendar Dec-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANSGAR MEDIA, LLC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Defendant Ansgar Media, LLC's petition to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is denied. The dispute is outside the scope of the arbitration clause in the Operating Agreement, which provides that "any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be arbitrated under the terms of this clause." This clause is limited in scope to disputes relating to the interpretation and performance of the Operating Agreement. (See Rice v. Downs (2016) 248 Cal.App.4th 175, 187.) Plaintiff's "Complaint for Breach of Written Loan Agreement" does not allege any breach of the Operating Agreement, or even mention that agreement. Rather, it sets forth causes of action for breach of four separate loan agreements that the parties entered into contemporaneously with the Operating Agreement. Critically, those agreements, each of which contains an integration clause, make no reference to arbitration. Rather, they explicitly contemplate that "the indebtedness represented by this Loan" may be collected "in bankruptcy, receivership or other judicial proceedings"; they contain a "governing law" clause by which each party "irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of any federal or state court sitting in the County of Nassau, State of New York, for the adjudication of any dispute hereunder or in connection herewith or with any transaction contemplated hereby or discussed herein," and by which each party "irrevocably waives, and agrees not to assert in any suit, action or proceeding, any claim that it is not personally subject to the jurisdiction of any such court, that such suit, action or proceeding is brought in an inconvenient forum or that the venue of such suit, action or proceeding is improper"; and they contain an explicit waiver of jury trial. Such explicit provisions are irreconcilable with an intent by the parties to arbitrate claims arising out of a breach of the loan agreements. (See id. at 185 ["In determining the scope of an arbitration clause, '[t]he court should attempt to give effect to the parties' intentions, in light of the usual and ordinary meaning of the contractual language and the circumstances under which the agreement was made'"]; see also id. at 191-192 [causes of action did not "arise out of" operating agreements merely because, but for those agreements, they would not exist].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
